claim: No Stable, release, conformance, package-parity, or recorded manual assistive-technology claim is made by this source contract.

semantics.nativeBasis: React Aria Slider with two or more visually hidden input type=range controls and one slider accessibility node per thumb
semantics.naming: Field names the slider group; thumbLabels provide distinct boundary names such as Minimum allocation and Maximum allocation.
semantics.roleOverride: Consumer role overrides are outside the supported contract.
semantics.refTarget: HTMLDivElement visual root; the first hidden input receives the integrated Field control ID

interaction.keyboard: Each thumb independently supports Arrow keys, Page Up, Page Down, Home, and End within the domain clamped by neighboring values.
interaction.pointer: A track press selects the nearest editable thumb and drag updates that thumb without swapping its identity.
interaction.touch: Every thumb and the shared track preserve a 44 CSS pixel target and support cancellable touch movement.
interaction.speech: Distinct visible group and thumb names allow boundary-specific voice targeting.
interaction.switchAccess: Every boundary is a separate focus stop with the complete keyboard path.

formBehavior: When names are supplied, each hidden native range input is a distinct successful control with a canonical ASCII number. Native reset restores every uncontrolled default; disabled ranges are omitted; read-only ranges remain successful.

states.synchronization: Output, selected fill, thumb positions, accessible value text, form values, read-only description, and callbacks share one ordered React Aria state engine.

preferences.enhancedContrast: Uses semantic tokens, geometry, and non-color invalid, read-only, and focus cues.
preferences.forcedColors: Uses system colors, explicit track and thumb borders, and a system-color focus indicator.
preferences.reducedMotion: Thumb transitions are removed and no boundary change depends on animation.

responsive.overflowPolicy: The horizontal track shrinks to its container; intermediate mark labels hide on narrow canvases while endpoints and every thumb remain operable.

internationalization.imePolicy: The controls do not accept free-form text and have no IME editing path.
internationalization.serialization: Visible and accessible values use Intl formatting; each submitted boundary remains a canonical JavaScript number.

consumerResponsibilities: Provide a persistent group label and distinct boundary labels that describe each thumb's meaning rather than only its order.
Supply equally sized value, thumbLabels, and names arrays when custom multi-thumb or form behavior is used.
Keep values ordered. Thumbs may collide but never cross or exchange semantic identity.
Use separate numeric fields when users must directly enter exact boundaries.
limitations: The supported collision policy clamps neighboring thumbs and allows equality; crossing and semantic thumb reordering are intentionally unsupported.
Source-present-unreleased records do not include immutable generated artifacts, packed-consumer evidence, package/source parity, or current manual assistive-technology sessions.
